human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for crisis-specific coordination behavior, confidentiality and safety boundaries, authority-source coverage, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness against issue #1128.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to behavioral-health crisis coordination, explicit ownership transfer, blocker tracking, crisis-continuum terminology, privacy-aware routing, and closure confirmation rather than generic care coordination.

Authority sources cover SAMHSA crisis guidance and model definitions, CMS mobile-crisis guidance, SAMHSA CCBHC criteria, 988 Lifeline minimum standards, HHS HIPAA mental-health sharing guidance, and 42 CFR Part 2.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, clinical-authority rejection, terminology normalization, low-confidence handling, policy conflicts, privacy escalation, payer-boundary routing, closure confirmation, anti-generic boundary enforcement, and warm-handoff blockers.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/behavioral-health-and-community-care/crisis-services-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent clinical, dispatch, privacy, payer, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated crisis services coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, privacy-aware routing, and closure confirmation across the behavioral-health crisis continuum.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
Functionality coverage is complete and aligned with evaluation/results.json.
